assertion_results.text: Step 7 Case A creates standard remediation tasks for the current stream (rhtpa-2.2) with standard labels ['ai-generated-jira', 'Security', 'CVE-2026-55123'] and 'Depend' link to TC-8020
assertion_results.evidence: In remediation.md, Case A creates two tasks for stream rhtpa-2.2. Task 1 (upstream backport) uses labels: ["ai-generated-jira", "Security", "CVE-2026-55123"] (line 23) and links to TC-8020 with type "Depend" (lines 73-80: jira.create_link(inwardIssue: "TC-8020", outwardIssue: <upstream-task-key>, type: "Depend")). Task 2 (downstream propagation) uses the same labels (line 89) and also links to TC-8020 with type "Depend" (lines 148-151). cross-stream.md Link Summary table confirms: TC-8020 -> upstream-task (rhtpa-2.2) = Depend, TC-8020 -> downstream-task (rhtpa-2.2) = Depend.

assertion_results.text: Step 7 Case B detects that stream rhtpa-2.1 is also affected but has no CVE Jira — creates proactive preemptive remediation tasks (§1.60)
assertion_results.evidence: In remediation.md lines 156-158: 'Cross-stream version impact analysis shows that stream rhtpa-2.1 also ships tokio 1.40.0, which is within the affected range (< 1.42.0). A JQL search for sibling CVE Jiras with label CVE-2026-55123 returns no results for stream rhtpa-2.1 -- no CVE Jira exists for that stream.' It then creates two preemptive remediation tasks (Preemptive Task 1 on line 162 and Preemptive Task 2 on line 238). cross-stream.md line 11 confirms: rhtpa-2.1 CVE Jira exists? = NO. The version-impact.md line 30 also states: 'Stream rhtpa-2.1 is outside the scope of TC-8020 but is also affected. This triggers Case B (cross-stream impact / proactive remediation) in Step 7.'

assertion_results.text: Preemptive tasks for stream rhtpa-2.1 include the 'security-preemptive' label alongside standard labels: ['ai-generated-jira', 'Security', 'CVE-2026-55123', 'security-preemptive']
assertion_results.evidence: In remediation.md, Preemptive Task 1 (line 172): labels: ["ai-generated-jira", "Security", "CVE-2026-55123", "security-preemptive"]. Preemptive Task 2 (line 249): labels: ["ai-generated-jira", "Security", "CVE-2026-55123", "security-preemptive"]. cross-stream.md also confirms in the table for both preemptive tasks: Labels = 'ai-generated-jira, Security, CVE-2026-55123, security-preemptive'.

assertion_results.text: Preemptive tasks are linked to the originating CVE Jira TC-8020 with 'Related' link type (not 'Depend') because the originating CVE belongs to a different stream
assertion_results.evidence: In remediation.md, Preemptive Task 1 link (lines 228-233): jira.create_link(inwardIssue: "TC-8020", outwardIssue: <preemptive-upstream-task-key>, type: "Related"). Preemptive Task 2 link (lines 305-309): jira.create_link(inwardIssue: "TC-8020", outwardIssue: <preemptive-downstream-task-key>, type: "Related"). cross-stream.md Link Summary confirms: TC-8020 -> preemptive-upstream-task (rhtpa-2.1) = Related, TC-8020 -> preemptive-downstream-task (rhtpa-2.1) = Related. cross-stream.md line 106 explicitly states: 'Preemptive tasks use "Related" links to TC-8020 (not "Depend"), because TC-8020 belongs to a different stream.'

assertion_results.text: Preemptive task descriptions include a preemptive remediation prefix noting TC-8020 (stream rhtpa-2.2) as the originating CVE
assertion_results.evidence: In remediation.md, Preemptive Task 1 description (lines 189-192): '> **Preemptive remediation**: This task was created proactively from cross-stream > impact analysis of TC-8020 (stream rhtpa-2.2). > No stream-specific CVE Jira exists yet for this stream. When PSIRT creates one, > this task will be linked and the `security-preemptive` label removed.' Preemptive Task 2 description (lines 265-268) contains the identical prefix. Both explicitly reference TC-8020 and stream rhtpa-2.2 as the originating CVE.
